system: Loopforge public catalog and loop distribution
description: Static GitHub Pages site serving catalog.json, loop prompts, and verifiers. Contributors change loops/ and catalog.json via git; users clone or fetch the catalog and run verify.py locally against artifacts an agent produces in their own workspace. Assumptions: no server-side execution on Pages; CI runs only on GitHub Actions; users treat prompt.md as untrusted reference per Learn page; no secrets in the repository.

assets.name: User workspace and source code under review

entry_points.name: Public HTTP fetch of site, catalog.json, and loop files
Pull request or push changing loops/ or catalog.json
Agent reads prompt.md and writes THREAT_MODEL.json locally
GitHub Actions runs ci/check.py on merge candidate

threats.title: Attacker serves tampered catalog.json or verifier via compromised Pages deploy pointing users at malicious loop definitions
Malicious pull request weakens verify.py so failing artifacts pass the gate
Prompt injection in prompt.md instructs agent to exfiltrate confidential workspace files while threat modeling
User treats catalog prompt text as authorization to run destructive commands in production
Catalog index advertises verifier path that does not exist, breaking automated loop discovery
Denial of service via oversized loop artifact or verifier run exhausting CI minutes
Unauthenticated visitor scrapes catalog.json to map security workflow patterns across targets

mitigations.description: Require maintainer review on loops/, ci/, and catalog.json changes before merge to main
ci/check.py runs check_loops.py and check_catalog.py on every pull request via GitHub Actions
Loop prompts state catalog content is untrusted reference data, not authorization to act
Learn page requires approval for consequential actions; verifier proves structure only
CI uses fixed example artifacts only; no arbitrary user uploads in the workflow
Accepted risk: catalog is public by design for machine discovery
